comments.text: The conjecture that integers of the form $p+2^k+2^l$ appear in every arithmetic progression of odd numbers, can be stated somewhat differently, as Erdos did. Let $p_{1},...,p_{n}$ be the first $n$ odd primes. Then there is a $N(n)$ such that for all $N>N(n)$, there exist $k,l$, with $2^k+2^l<N$ and $N-2^k-2^l$ is not divisible by any of the $p_{i}$, for $i =1,...n$.
